logline: A community of craftspeople and educators deliberately chooses analog methods, finding that imperfection carries a meaning that optimization erases.
scene: a woodworking workshop in rural Mexico, natural light pouring through open barn doors, a woman in a work apron hand-planing a piece of wood, sawdust floating in the light beams, traditional hand tools organized on a pegboard wall, a half-finished wooden chair on a workbench, no screens or digital devices visible, warm but desaturated
keyProps: Hand plane shaping wood with floating sawdust
Natural light pouring through open barn doors
Traditional hand tools on a pegboard wall
A half-finished wooden chair on a workbench
camera: Wide workspace framing, 35mm lens, natural light emphasis, documentary style
lighting: Natural light pouring through open barn doors creating visible light beams with floating sawdust, warm but desaturated overall tone
productionDesign: Warm but desaturated palette, 35mm film look, rural Mexican woodworking workshop, intentionally analog environment with no digital technology visible
signatureTwist: close-up of a hand-made wooden chair with a slightly crooked leg, the wood grain visible and beautiful, cool window light falling across the chair casting shadows that reveal the imperfections, a perfectly manufactured chair of similar design visible out of focus in the background for contrast, the handmade chair in sharp focus
